title: cracca apollinea
intro: Cracca apollinea, also known as Coronilla valentina subsp. glauca, is a flowering plant species in the legume family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cracca apollinea include its ability to support urinary system health and relieve symptoms associated with urinary tract infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, often in combination with other herbs, to leverage its medicinal properties.
